Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Transfer Action And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-28-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Rachel S. Silverman, Esq. of Voss Silverman & Braybrooke LLP, Tel. 310.306.0515, for defendant and moving party, by Court Call;
Ronald K. Losch, Esq. of Losch & Ehrlich, Tel. 415.956.8400, for plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The Court did not allow argument because defendant's counsel did not give timely notice that she is opposing the tentative ruling. Therefore, the tentative ruling was adopted as the ruling of the Court. DEFENDANT PREFERRED CONVENTION SERVICES, INC.'S Motion To Transfer Action And For Sanctions is denied. An action on a contract is triable in the county where the contract was "in fact" entered into. (CCP 395(a).) Defendant has not sustained its burden to transfer the action. Mr. Chaney's office was in San Francisco at the time the contract was signed. (Zabit Decl. 5.) The contract, which includes the MSA and the Telekenex Information Services Supplement ("ISS") (both attached as an exhibit to the Complaint), shows that Mr. Chaney signed the ISS on January 4, 2012 as CEI of Telekenex, and the address of Telekenex is shown in San Francisco, CA. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.
